title: Adder-Substractor inference
body:
Adder-Subtractor inference is one of common transformations done by synthesis tools.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adder%E2%80%93subtractor

From this Verilog:
```verilog
assign res = sel
  ? (a - b)
  : (a + b);
```
Full version: https://github.com/drom/icicles/blob/master/src/add_sub.v

Synthesizer can infer something like this:

```verilog
assign sum = {a, sel} + {(sel ? ~b : b), 1'b1};
assign res = sum[W:1];
```
Full version: https://github.com/drom/icicles/blob/master/src/add_sub_opt.v

Second version requires ~2x less LUTs and faster too.

Does such optimizations belongs to Yosys project?
code_blocks:
assign sum = {a, sel} + {(sel ? ~b : b), 1'b1};
assign res = sum[W:1];
